identity.name: Telegram Logger Bot
identity.purpose: Asynchronous system designed for monitoring, filtering, and persisting Telegram message data, media metadata, and user/chat interactions into a structured SQLite database.

components.file: Initializes the Dispatcher, handles bot polling, and implements high-level access control. Directs unauthorized chats to a "leave" procedure.
Loads environment variables and JSON configurations. Normalizes Telegram Chat IDs to the standard Bot API format (-100...).
Contains logic for parsing incoming Message and ChatMemberUpdated objects. Extracts text, media metadata (photos, videos, documents, voice), and forward origins.
1. Defines the SQLite schema. 2. Executes CRUD operations using UPSERT patterns to maintain data consistency for users and chats. 3. Provides complex data aggregation logic for reporting and exports.
CLI tool for exporting stored data. Supports JSON format for programmatic use and HTML format for human-readable debugging. Delegates data retrieval and relational aggregation to database.py.

table.name: users
table.fields: id (PK), username, first_name, last_name, is_bot, language_code, updated_at.

table.name: chats
table.description: Stores Telegram groups, supergroups, channels, and private chats.
table.fields: id (PK), type, title, username, description, updated_at.

table.name: messages
table.description: Primary log of all captured messages.
table.fields: id (PK), tg_id, chat_id (FK), sender_id, reply_to_local_id, forward_sender_id, forward_message_id, forward_sender_name, text, entities (JSON), media_group_id, date, edit_date.

table.name: message_media
table.description: Metadata for files attached to messages.
table.fields: id (PK), message_id (FK), file_id, file_unique_id, file_type, file_size, mime_type, width, height.

table.name: chat_members
table.description: Intersection table tracking user activity and status within specific chats.
table.fields: chat_id (PK), user_id (PK), status, joined_at, left_at, first_activity, last_activity, updated_at.

access_control.private_chats: Only users defined in ALLOWED_USERS are processed.
access_control.group_chats: Only chat IDs defined in ALLOWED_CHATS are processed. Unauthorized groups trigger an automated "leave chat" sequence.

logic_flows.message_processing: 1. Validate source chat/user against whitelist. 2. Upsert chat and sender (user or sender_chat) info. 3. Resolve local ID of the replied-to message if applicable. 4. Parse forward origins (user, hidden user, chat, or channel). 5. Persist message text and entities. 6. Extract and store metadata for all media attachments. 7. Update member activity timestamps.

data_export.retrieval: Data aggregation is handled by the persistence layer to ensure consistency between the bot and maintenance tools.
data_export.json_format: Flat representation of database tables for data migration or external processing.
data_export.html_format: Relational representation for debugging: - Users: Aggregated with their chat memberships and status. - Chats: Aggregated with the list of participating users. - Timestamps: Converted from Unix epoch to human-readable UTC format.

operational_notes.note: The system ignores messages from unauthorized sources silently or with a farewell message depending on chat type.
Media files are not downloaded; only their metadata and Telegram file_ids are stored.
SQLite WAL mode is used to ensure concurrency during polling and external dumping.
The HTML export format is strictly for manual debugging and audit, utilizing basic formatting without external dependencies (CSS/JS).
